metadata.title: China EV ecosystem + the EV transition — BYD/NIO/XPeng/Xiaomi, CATL batteries, the price war + tariff fight, and AV/robotaxi (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the makers, BYD's lead, CATL's battery share, the EU/US tariffs, and the AV/robotaxi leaders are FACT (company/customs/regulator data, news of record); the 'overcapacity/subsidy dumping' vs 'genuine cost leadership/protectionism' tariff debate is presented BOTH ways with dates
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the auto epic (#177). China's EV bloc — now the world's largest — plus the battery layer (CATL), the brutal domestic price war + overcapacity, the Western tariff response, and the AV/robotaxi race. Makers: BYD (global #1), SAIC/MG, NIO/XPeng/Li Auto, Xiaomi EV, Chery, Great Wall, Geely (cross-link #180), and Leapmotor (Stellantis JV). CATL anchors batteries. The tariff fight (EU CVD + US 100%) is the geo-economic flashpoint. AV: Waymo (US) vs Baidu Apollo Go (China) vs Tesla robotaxi. Attaches to China, Geely, Tesla, EU, US_Government, Stellantis, Ford, NACS.

the_makers: China's EV/NEV makers: BYD — the GLOBAL #1 (vertically integrated: its own Blade LFP batteries + chips; overtook Tesla in quarterly BEV deliveries 2023-24; Berkshire Hathaway, an early backer, trimmed/exited its stake 2022-25); SAIC (state-owned; the MG brand; the EU's largest CVD-tariff target); the 'new forces' NIO / XPeng / Li Auto; Xiaomi EV (the phone giant's SU7, launched 2024); Chery (China's top exporter) and Great Wall Motor; plus Geely (Zeekr/Polestar). grade: fact.
the_price_war: The domestic stress: a brutal multi-year PRICE WAR (BYD-led, 2023-25) amid OVERCAPACITY (industry utilization well below 100%), forcing consolidation and razor margins; in 2025 Beijing itself pushed back against destructive 'involution'/price-cutting. grade: fact.
the_batteries: The battery layer: CATL is the world's #1 maker (~37% global share), with BYD #2; LG Energy Solution, Panasonic, Samsung SDI, SK On, and CALB round out the field. CATL's reach is contentious in the West: it LICENSED its LFP technology to Ford's Marshall, Michigan plant (announced 2023, later downsized amid political pushback over Chinese-tech dependence). Battery demand cross-links the critical-minerals/Lobito threads (lithium/cobalt/nickel). grade: fact.
the_tariffs: The geo-economic flashpoint, both ways with dates: (A) WESTERN response — the EU imposed COUNTERVAILING DUTIES on Chinese BEVs (finalized Oct 2024, up to ~35% on top of the 10% base; e.g. SAIC ~35%, Geely ~19%, BYD ~17%), the US set a 100% EV tariff (2024), and Canada matched — citing state SUBSIDIES + OVERCAPACITY 'dumping.' (B) CHINA framing — these are PROTECTIONISM against genuine cost/scale leadership, and China retaliated (anti-dumping probes on EU brandy/dairy/pork). The honest middle: Chinese subsidies AND real cost leadership both exist; the Mexico-assembly 'backdoor' is a live concern. grade: both documented.
the_av_robotaxi: The autonomy race (separate from the makers): WAYMO (Alphabet) leads US paid robotaxi (multi-city); BAIDU's Apollo Go leads China (scaled in Wuhan, 2024); Tesla launched an Austin robotaxi (2025); GM's Cruise wound down (2024, see #179); Pony.ai/WeRide are other Chinese players; Mobileye (Intel) supplies ADAS. grade: fact.
the_two_sides: Both ways, dated: (A) CHINA-AHEAD framing — China built the dominant EV + battery supply chain at lower cost, BYD leads globally, and Apollo Go scaled robotaxi; Western tariffs are defensive protectionism. (B) STRESS/SECURITY framing — a destructive price war + overcapacity strain even Chinese makers, Western markets are walling off Chinese EVs/batteries on subsidy + data-security grounds, and CATL dependence is politically fraught (the Ford license fight). grade: both documented.
the_honest_reading: China owns the EV + battery scale story (BYD global #1; CATL ~37% of batteries; the largest EV market) — built on a mix of REAL cost leadership AND state subsidies, both true, which is exactly why the West's tariff response (EU CVD + US 100%, 2024) is itself dual-natured (legitimate anti-subsidy + protectionism). The domestic price war/overcapacity is a genuine stress even for the winners. On autonomy, Waymo (US) and Apollo Go (China) lead paid robotaxi while Tesla pushes FSD. Tariff intent and 'who's ahead' are presented both ways with dates. Cross-links China, Geely, Tesla, the Western governments, and the critical-minerals/Lobito battery-input threads. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
